message.content:
For PR #348, find all relevant CLAUDE.md files.

First, get the list of modified file paths from the PR using:
`gh pr view 348 --json files --jq '.files[].path'`

Then find:
1. Root CLAUDE.md (if exists)
2. Any CLAUDE.md files in directories that contain modified files

Return a list of CLAUDE.md file paths found. Use Glob or Read tools to check if files exist.

Be concise - just return the list of paths.

1â†’# CLAUDE.md
     2â†’
     3â†’This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
     4â†’
     5â†’## Tools & Requirements
     6â†’
     7â†’- **Node.js**: >=v22.14.0 (see `.node-version`, `.nvmrc`)
     8â†’- **npm**: >=10.9.2
     9â†’- **Core stack**: NestJS, Fastify, MySQL, Knex.js, TypeScript (strict mode)
    10â†’- **Testing**: Jest (unit/e2e), Artillery (load testing)
    11â†’- **Code quality**: ESLint v9+ (flat config), Prettier, Husky
    12â†’- **Monitoring**: New Relic, Rollbar
    13â†’- **CLI**: nest-commander
    14â†’- **Cloud**: AWS SDK (SQS), PostgreSQL, Redis (ioredis), Redlock
    15â†’- **AI/ML**: OpenAI API integration
    16â†’- **Search**: Algolia
    17â†’
    18â†’## Key Commands
    19â†’
    20â†’```bash
    21â†’# Development
    22â†’npm run start:dev          # Hot reload
    23â†’npm run ًںگژ                 # Hot reload (horse emoji shortcut)
    24â†’npm run build              # Production build
    25â†’npm run start:prod         # Production start
    26â†’
    27â†’# Testing
    28â†’npm run test               # Full suite (lint, format, type-check, unit, e2e)
    29â†’npm run test:unit          # Unit tests only
    30â†’npm run test:e2e          # E2E tests (auto DB reset)
    31â†’npm run test:coverage      # Full suite with coverage reports
    32â†’npm run test:unit:coverage # Unit tests with coverage
    33â†’npm run test:e2e:coverage  # E2E tests with coverage
    34â†’
    35â†’# Database
    36â†’npm run migrate            # Run pending migrations
    37â†’npm run migrate:prod       # Production migrations
    38â†’npm run migrate:make name  # Create migration
    39â†’npm run migrate:up         # Migrate one up
    40â†’npm run migrate:down       # Migrate one down
    41â†’npm run migrate:rollback   # Rollback last batch
    42â†’npm run db:reset:test     # Reset test DB
    43â†’
    44â†’# Code Quality
    45â†’npm run code-quality-check # Run lint, format, and type-check
    46â†’npm run lint              # Check linting
    47â†’npm run lint:fix          # Auto-fix linting
    48â†’npm run format            # Format code
    49â†’npm run type-check        # TypeScript check
    50â†’
    51â†’# CLI & Load Testing
    52â†’npm run cli:dev           # Run CLI commands (dev)
    53â†’npm run cli:prod          # Run CLI commands (prod)
    54â†’npm run artillery-load-test  # Load testing
    55â†’```
    56â†’
    57â†’## Architecture
    58â†’
    59â†’**NestJS microservice** with MySQL replication, strict TypeScript, modular domain organization, AWS SQS integration, Redis caching, and AI-powered recommendations.
    60â†’
    61â†’### Database Pattern
    62â†’
    63â†’```typescript
    64â†’constructor(
    65â†’  @InjectConnection(KNEX_READ_CONNECTION) private readonly knexRead: Knex,
    66â†’  @InjectConnection(KNEX_WRITE_CONNECTION) private readonly knexWrite: Knex,
    67â†’) {}
    68â†’```
    69â†’
    70â†’- Use `knexRead` for SELECT queries
    71â†’- Use `knexWrite` for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations
    72â†’
    73â†’### Project Structure
    74â†’
    75â†’```
    76â†’src/
    77â†’â”œâ”€â”€ modules/
    78â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ rest/              # API endpoints
    79â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ addons/       # Product addon recommendations
    80â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ email-recommendations/  # Email-based recommendations
    81â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ frequently-bought-together/  # FBT recommendations
    82â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ load-test/    # Load testing endpoints
    83â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ open-ai/      # OpenAI integration
    84â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ process-style/ # Style processing
    85â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promos/       # Promotional content
    86â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promotional-products/  # Promo product recommendations
    87â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ related-products/  # Related product recommendations
    88â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ robots/       # Robots.txt
    89â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-products/  # Trending product recommendations
    90â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ db/               # Database models & services
    91â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ category-correlations/
    92â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ condensed-features/
    93â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ forecasted-trending-products/
    94â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ product-annual-sales/
    95â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promotional-products/
    96â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ redshift/     # Redshift data integration
    97â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ orders/
    98â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-items/
    99â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ root-categories-correlations/
   100â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ style-colors/
   101â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ styles/
   102â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ styles-hash/
   103â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ cli/              # CLI commands
   104â†’â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ commands/     # Command implementations
   105â†’â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ workers/      # Background workers
   106â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ algolia/          # Algolia search integration
   107â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ cache/            # Redis caching
   108â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ health/           # Health checks
   109â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ mms/              # MMS integration
   110â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ mms-styles-sync/  # MMS style synchronization
   111â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ newrelic/         # New Relic monitoring
   112â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ env-config/       # Environment configuration
   113â†’â”œâ”€â”€ dto/                  # Data Transfer Objects by feature
   114â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ addons/
   115â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ brand-products/
   116â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ email-recommendations/
   117â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ frequently-bought-together/
   118â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ load-test/
   119â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ nlp-features/
   120â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ related-products/
   121â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-products/
   122â†’â”œâ”€â”€ utils/                # Utility functions
   123â†’â”œâ”€â”€ middlewares/          # Custom middlewares
   124â†’â”œâ”€â”€ interfaces/           # TypeScript interfaces
   125â†’â”œâ”€â”€ decorators/           # Custom decorators
   126â†’â”œâ”€â”€ interceptors/         # Custom interceptors
   127â†’â”œâ”€â”€ guards/               # Auth guards
   128â†’â”œâ”€â”€ constants/            # Constants and enums
   129â†’â”œâ”€â”€ configs/              # Configuration files
   130â†’â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/              # Automation & evaluation scripts
   131â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ evaluator-related-products/
   132â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ evaluator-fbt/
   133â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ load-testing/
   134â†’â”œâ”€â”€ migrations/           # Database migrations
   135â†’â”œâ”€â”€ seeds/                # Database seeds
   136â†’â””â”€â”€ unit-tests/          # Unit tests
   137â†’test/                    # E2E tests
   138â†’```
   139â†’
   140â†’### Import Aliases (tsconfig.json)
   141â†’
   142â†’```typescript
   143â†’@config/*        â†’ src/configs/*
   144â†’@constants/*     â†’ src/constants/*
   145â†’@modules/*       â†’ src/modules/*
   146â†’@utils/*         â†’ src/utils/*
   147â†’@middlewares/*   â†’ src/middlewares/*
   148â†’@services/*      â†’ src/services/*
   149â†’@interfaces/*    â†’ src/interfaces/*
   150â†’@decorators/*    â†’ src/decorators/*
   151â†’@dto/*           â†’ src/dto/*
   152â†’@guards/*        â†’ src/guards/*
   153â†’@interceptors/*  â†’ src/interceptors/*
   154â†’@frontend-tests/* â†’ src/frontend-tests/*
   155â†’@seeds/*         â†’ src/seeds/*
   156â†’```
   157â†’
   158â†’### Database Models
   159â†’
   160â†’The service includes comprehensive database models with full CRUD operations:
   161â†’
   162â†’- **styles**: Core product styling data with UUID primary key
   163â†’- **style_colors**: Color information (style_id, color_id, color_rgb, color_name)
   164â†’- **styles_hash**: Hash-based style identification (style_id, hash, hashing_algorithm)
   165â†’- **condensed_features**: Compressed feature data (style_id, condensed_features JSON)
   166â†’- **category_correlations**: Category relationship data (primary_category_id, edge_id, edge_weight)
   167â†’- **root_categories_correlations**: Root-level category relationships
   168â†’- **forecasted_trending_products**: ML-forecasted trending products
   169â†’- **product_annual_sales**: Annual sales data for products
   170â†’- **promotional_products**: Promotional product data
   171â†’- **redshift_orders**: Order data from Redshift
   172â†’- **redshift_trending_items**: Trending items data from Redshift
   173â†’
   174â†’Each model includes:
   175â†’
   176â†’- Base, Create, and Update DTOs in `/dto/` folders or module folders
   177â†’- TypeScript interfaces in `/interfaces/` folders
   178â†’- Service layer with read/write database separation
   179â†’- Soft deletes with `deleted_at` timestamp (where applicable)
   180â†’- Proper indexing and unique constraints
   181â†’
   182â†’### CLI Commands
   183â†’
   184â†’Available CLI commands (via `npm run cli:dev`):
   185â†’
   186â†’- **Migration Commands**: `migrate`, `migrate-up`, `migrate-down`, `migrate-rollback`
   187â†’- **Data Population**: `populate-category-correlations`, `populate-trending-items`
   188â†’- **Forecasting**: `forecast-trending-products`
   189â†’- **Synchronization**: `sync-source-styles`
   190â†’- **Data Preparation**: `prepare-promo-seed`
   191â†’
   192â†’## Development Rules
   193â†’
   194â†’### Code Style
   195â†’
   196â†’- **NO COMMENTS**, **NO EXPLANATIONS** in code (except ESLint disable commands)
   197â†’- **ALWAYS INSPECT EXISTING FILES** before creating new ones
   198â†’- Match exact patterns from existing files:
   199â†’  - `.model.ts` â†’ check `/src/modules/db/*/models/`
   200â†’  - `.service.ts` â†’ check similar modules
   201â†’  - `.controller.ts` â†’ check `/src/modules/rest/`
   202â†’  - `.dto.ts` â†’ check `/src/dto/` or module folders
   203â†’  - `.util.ts` â†’ check `/src/utils/`
   204â†’  - `.unit-spec.ts` â†’ check `/src/unit-tests/`
   205â†’  - `.e2e-spec.ts` â†’ check `/test/`
   206â†’  - `.interface.ts` â†’ check `/src/interfaces/`
   207â†’
   208â†’### Environment Variables (CRITICAL)
   209â†’
   210â†’**ALWAYS follow this complete process when adding new environment variables:**
   211â†’
   212â†’#### Step 1: Add to Environment Files
   213â†’
   214â†’Add the new variable to env files with documentation:
   215â†’
   216â†’```bash
   217â†’# .env
   218â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
   219â†’```
   220â†’
   221â†’```bash
   222â†’# .env.test
   223â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
   224â†’```
   225â†’
   226â†’```bash
   227â†’# .env.example
   228â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
   229â†’```
   230â†’
   231â†’```bash
   232â†’# .env.test.example
   233â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=test_value # Use appropriate test value
   234â†’```
   235â†’
   236â†’#### Step 2: Create Constant Export
   237â†’
   238â†’Add constant to `src/constants/env-variables.constant.ts`:
   239â†’
   240â†’```typescript
   241â†’export const NEW_VARIABLE_NAME = 'NEW_VARIABLE_NAME';
   242â†’```
   243â†’
   244â†’#### Step 3: Add Validation Rule
   245â†’
   246â†’Add validation decorator to `src/configs/env-validations.config.ts` in the `EnvironmentVariables` class:
   247â†’
   248â†’```typescript
   249â†’@IsString() // Use appropriate validator: @IsString(), @IsNumber(), @IsBoolean(), @IsEnum()
   250â†’@IsNotEmpty() // Add constraints: @Min(), @Max(), etc.
   251â†’NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: string; // Use appropriate type
   252â†’```
   253â†’
   254â†’**Common validators:**
   255â†’
   256â†’- `@IsString()` - String values
   257â†’- `@IsNumber()` - Numeric values
   258â†’- `@IsBoolean()` - Boolean values
   259â†’- `@IsEnum(EnumType)` - Enum values
   260â†’- `@IsNotEmpty()` - Required field
   261â†’- `@Min(value)` / `@Max(value)` - Numeric constraints
   262â†’- `@IsUrl()` - URL validation
   263â†’
   264â†’#### Step 4: Add to Environment Loader
   265â†’
   266â†’Add to `src/utils/get-env-file.util.ts` in the `getEnvVariables()` function:
   267â†’
   268â†’```typescript
   269â†’export const getEnvVariables = (): Record<string, string | number | undefined> => ({
   270â†’	// ... existing variables
   271â†’	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: process.env['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
   272â†’});
   273â†’```
   274â†’
   275â†’#### Step 5: Add Test Coverage
   276â†’
   277â†’Add test cases to `test/env-config.module.e2e-spec.ts`:
   278â†’
   279â†’```typescript
   280â†’// In beforeAll hook
   281â†’testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'] = String(envConfig['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME']);
   282â†’
   283â†’// In test case
   284â†’it('should load NEW_VARIABLE_NAME correctly', () => {
   285â†’	expect(envConfigService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME })).toBe(
   286â†’		testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
   287â†’	);
   288â†’});
   289â†’```
   290â†’
   291â†’#### Usage: Two Approaches
   292â†’
   293â†’**Approach 1: EnvConfigService (Dependency Injection)**
   294â†’
   295â†’```typescript
   296â†’import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
   297â†’import { EnvConfigService } from '@modules/env-config/env-config.service';
   298â†’import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
   299â†’
   300â†’@Injectable()
   301â†’export class MyService {
   302â†’	constructor(private readonly configService: EnvConfigService) {}
   303â†’
   304â†’	someMethod(): void {
   305â†’		const value = this.configService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
   306â†’		const numericValue = this.configService.get<number>({
   307â†’			key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME,
   308â†’			converter: Number,
   309â†’		});
   310â†’	}
   311â†’}
   312â†’```
   313â†’
   314â†’**Approach 2: getEnv Utility (Standalone Usage)**
   315â†’
   316â†’```typescript
   317â†’import { getEnv } from '@utils/get-env-variable.util';
   318â†’import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';
   319â†’
   320â†’const value = getEnv<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
   321â†’const numericValue = getEnv<number>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME, converter: Number });
   322â†’```
   323â†’
   324â†’**Important Notes:**
   325â†’
   326â†’- **ALWAYS** complete ALL 5 steps - missing any step will cause validation failures
   327â†’- **NEVER** access `process.env` directly in application code
   328â†’- **ALWAYS** use constants from `@constants/env-variables.constant`
   329â†’- **TEST** your changes by running `npm run test:e2e`
   330â†’- Validation runs automatically on app startup and will fail fast if misconfigured
   331â†’- For production, environment variables are loaded from system environment
   332â†’- For dev/test, variables are loaded from `.env` and `.env.test` files
   333â†’
   334â†’### Git Workflow
   335â†’
   336â†’- **Branches**: `RECO-XXXX-suffix`
   337â†’- **Commits**: `RECO-XXXX: Description in imperative mood`
   338â†’- **PRs**: `RECO-XXXX: Short description`, requires Code Owner approval, squash merge only
   339â†’
   340â†’### Common Patterns
   341â†’
   342â†’```typescript
   343â†’// Logging
   344â†’import { Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
   345â†’export class ClassName {
   346â†’  private readonly logger = new Logger(ClassName.name);
   347â†’}
   348â†’
   349â†’// Swagger
   350â†’@ApiOperation({ summary: 'Description' })
   351â†’@ApiResponse({ status: HttpStatusCode.Ok, description: 'Success response' })
   352â†’```
   353â†’
   354â†’### LogExecution Decorator (CRITICAL)
   355â†’
   356â†’**ALWAYS wrap important methods and 3rd party requests with `@LogExecution` decorator.**
   357â†’
   358â†’The `@LogExecution` decorator provides comprehensive logging and monitoring for method execution:
   359â†’
   360â†’- **Performance tracking**: Logs execution time with warnings if exceeds threshold
   361â†’- **Parameter logging**: Logs method input parameters
   362â†’- **Response logging**: Logs method output
   363â†’- **Error tracking**: Logs errors and reports to Rollbar
   364â†’- **Sensitive data masking**: Automatically masks passwords, tokens, auth keys
   365â†’- **Custom formatters**: Optional custom formatters for params/responses
   366â†’
   367â†’**Usage:**
   368â†’
   369â†’```typescript
   370â†’import { LogExecution } from '@decorators/log-execution.decorator';
   371â†’
   372â†’@Injectable()
   373â†’export class MyService {
   374â†’	// Basic usage - logs everything with defaults
   375â†’	@LogExecution()
   376â†’	async myMethod(param: string): Promise<Result> {
   377â†’		// method implementation
   378â†’	}
   379â†’
   380â†’	// With custom formatters (for large payloads or 3rd party responses)
   381â†’	@LogExecution({
   382â†’		paramFormatter: (params: { styleId: string }) => ({ styleId: params.styleId }),
   383â†’		responseFormatter: () => '', // Don't log large response
   384â†’	})
   385â†’	async fetchFromThirdParty({ styleId }: { styleId: string }): Promise<LargeData> {
   386â†’		// 3rd party API call
   387â†’	}
   388â†’
   389â†’	// With custom options
   390â†’	@LogExecution({
   391â†’		logParams: true,
   392â†’		logResponse: false,
   393â†’		logExecutionTime: true,
   394â†’		logErrToRollbar: true,
   395â†’		maskSensitiveData: true,
   396â†’	})
   397â†’	async sensitiveOperation(data: SensitiveData): Promise<void> {
   398â†’		// sensitive operation
   399â†’	}
   400â†’}
   401â†’```
   402â†’
   403â†’**When to use:**
   404â†’
   405â†’- âœ… All 3rd party API requests (Algolia, MMS, AWS, OpenAI, etc.)
   406â†’- âœ… Important business logic methods
   407â†’- âœ… Database operations that may be slow
   408â†’- âœ… Methods that handle sensitive data
   409â†’- âœ… Methods with complex error scenarios
   410â†’- â‌Œ Simple getters/setters
   411â†’- â‌Œ Private utility methods
   412â†’- â‌Œ Methods called in tight loops
   413â†’
   414â†’**Configuration options:**
   415â†’
   416â†’- `logParams`: Log method parameters (default: `true`)
   417â†’- `logResponse`: Log method response (default: `true`)
   418â†’- `logExecutionTime`: Log execution time (default: `true`)
   419â†’- `logErrToRollbar`: Log errors to Rollbar (default: `true`)
   420â†’- `rollbarLevel`: Rollbar log level (default: `'debug'`)
   421â†’- `maskSensitiveData`: Mask sensitive keys (default: `true`)
   422â†’- `sensitiveKeys`: Array of sensitive key names to mask
   423â†’- `customContext`: Additional context to include in logs
   424â†’- `paramFormatter`: Custom formatter function for parameters
   425â†’- `responseFormatter`: Custom formatter function for response
   426â†’
   427â†’**Default sensitive keys masked:**
   428â†’`password`, `pass`, `token`, `secret`, `auth`, `authorization`, `cookie`, `session`, `credentials`, `apiKey`, `_cookie`, `csrf`, `_csrf`
   429â†’
   430â†’## Configuration Files
   431â†’
   432â†’### TypeScript Configuration (tsconfig.json)
   433â†’
   434â†’- **Target**: ES2023 with strict type checking
   435â†’- **Strict options**: All enabled including `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`
   436â†’- **Path aliases**: Configured for clean imports (see Import Aliases section)
   437â†’- **Incremental compilation**: Enabled for faster builds
   438â†’- **strictPropertyInitialization**: Disabled (due to NestJS dependency injection)
   439â†’
   440â†’### ESLint Configuration (eslint.config.mjs)
   441â†’
   442â†’**When writing code, follow these ESLint rules:**
   443â†’
   444â†’- **No console**: Use Logger instead of `console.log`
   445â†’- **No relative imports**: Use path aliases only (`@modules/`, `@utils/`, etc.)
   446â†’- **Explicit return types**: All functions must have explicit return types (`@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types`)
   447â†’- **Import order**: Enforced with newlines between groups (builtin â†’ external â†’ internal â†’ type)
   448â†’- **TypeScript strict**: All strict TypeScript rules enabled
   449â†’- **Test files**: Relaxed rules for `*.spec.ts` files (allow `any`, relative imports)
   450â†’- **Import newlines**: Required after imports (`import/newline-after-import`)
   451â†’- **No duplicates**: Duplicate imports not allowed (`import/no-duplicates`)
   452â†’
   453â†’## Testing
   454â†’
   455â†’- **Unit**: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `/src/unit-tests/`
   456â†’- **E2E**: `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `/test/`
   457â†’- **Coverage**: Separate reports in `/coverage-unit/` and `/coverage-e2e/`
   458â†’- **DB**: MySQL replica with auto-reset for e2e tests
   459â†’- **Load Testing**: Artillery configuration in `/src/scripts/load-testing/`
   460â†’
   461â†’### Coverage Requirements
   462â†’
   463â†’All test suites must maintain **95% minimum coverage** across all metrics:
   464â†’
   465â†’- **Branches**: 95%
   466â†’- **Functions**: 95%
   467â†’- **Lines**: 95%
   468â†’- **Statements**: 95%
   469â†’
   470â†’**Configuration:**
   471â†’
   472â†’- Coverage thresholds enforced in `test/jest.coverage.config.ts`
   473â†’- Unit tests ignore `/modules/db/` patterns
   474â†’- E2E tests collect coverage only from `*.(controller|service).ts` files
   475â†’- Tests will fail if coverage falls below thresholds
   476â†’- Run `npm run test:coverage` to verify all coverage metrics
   477â†’
   478â†’## Key Dependencies
   479â†’
   480â†’### Core
   481â†’
   482â†’- **@nestjs/\*** - NestJS framework and modules
   483â†’- **@nestjs/platform-fastify** - Fastify adapter for performance
   484â†’- **knex** + **mysql2** - Database query builder and MySQL driver
   485â†’- **nest-knexjs** - Knex integration for NestJS
   486â†’
   487â†’### Caching & Queue
   488â†’
   489â†’- **ioredis** + **@keyv/redis** - Redis client and keyv adapter
   490â†’- **redlock** - Distributed locking
   491â†’- **@aws-sdk/client-sqs** + **sqs-consumer** - AWS SQS integration
   492â†’
   493â†’### Monitoring & Logging
   494â†’
   495â†’- **newrelic** - Application performance monitoring
   496â†’- **rollbar** - Error tracking
   497â†’- **correlation-id-ts** - Request correlation tracking
   498â†’
   499â†’### AI & Search
   500â†’
   501â†’- **openai** - OpenAI API integration
   502â†’- **algoliasearch** - Algolia search integration
   503â†’
   504â†’### Utilities
   505â†’
   506â†’- **class-transformer** + **class-validator** - DTO validation and transformation
   507â†’- **color-diff** - Color comparison utilities
   508â†’- **date-holidays** - Holiday date calculations
   509â†’

<system-reminder>
Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Tools & Requirements

- **Node.js**: >=v22.14.0 (see `.node-version`, `.nvmrc`)
- **npm**: >=10.9.2
- **Core stack**: NestJS, Fastify, MySQL, Knex.js, TypeScript (strict mode)
- **Testing**: Jest (unit/e2e), Artillery (load testing)
- **Code quality**: ESLint v9+ (flat config), Prettier, Husky
- **Monitoring**: New Relic, Rollbar
- **CLI**: nest-commander
- **Cloud**: AWS SDK (SQS), PostgreSQL, Redis (ioredis), Redlock
- **AI/ML**: OpenAI API integration
- **Search**: Algolia

## Key Commands

```bash
# Development
npm run start:dev          # Hot reload
npm run ًںگژ                 # Hot reload (horse emoji shortcut)
npm run build              # Production build
npm run start:prod         # Production start

# Testing
npm run test               # Full suite (lint, format, type-check, unit, e2e)
npm run test:unit          # Unit tests only
npm run test:e2e          # E2E tests (auto DB reset)
npm run test:coverage      # Full suite with coverage reports
npm run test:unit:coverage # Unit tests with coverage
npm run test:e2e:coverage  # E2E tests with coverage

# Database
npm run migrate            # Run pending migrations
npm run migrate:prod       # Production migrations
npm run migrate:make name  # Create migration
npm run migrate:up         # Migrate one up
npm run migrate:down       # Migrate one down
npm run migrate:rollback   # Rollback last batch
npm run db:reset:test     # Reset test DB

# Code Quality
npm run code-quality-check # Run lint, format, and type-check
npm run lint              # Check linting
npm run lint:fix          # Auto-fix linting
npm run format            # Format code
npm run type-check        # TypeScript check

# CLI & Load Testing
npm run cli:dev           # Run CLI commands (dev)
npm run cli:prod          # Run CLI commands (prod)
npm run artillery-load-test  # Load testing
```

## Architecture

**NestJS microservice** with MySQL replication, strict TypeScript, modular domain organization, AWS SQS integration, Redis caching, and AI-powered recommendations.

### Database Pattern

```typescript
constructor(
  @InjectConnection(KNEX_READ_CONNECTION) private readonly knexRead: Knex,
  @InjectConnection(KNEX_WRITE_CONNECTION) private readonly knexWrite: Knex,
) {}
```

- Use `knexRead` for SELECT queries
- Use `knexWrite` for INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE operations

### Project Structure

```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ modules/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ rest/              # API endpoints
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ addons/       # Product addon recommendations
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ email-recommendations/  # Email-based recommendations
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ frequently-bought-together/  # FBT recommendations
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ load-test/    # Load testing endpoints
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ open-ai/      # OpenAI integration
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ process-style/ # Style processing
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promos/       # Promotional content
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promotional-products/  # Promo product recommendations
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ related-products/  # Related product recommendations
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ robots/       # Robots.txt
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-products/  # Trending product recommendations
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ db/               # Database models & services
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ category-correlations/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ condensed-features/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ forecasted-trending-products/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ product-annual-sales/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ promotional-products/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ redshift/     # Redshift data integration
â”‚   â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ orders/
â”‚   â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-items/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ root-categories-correlations/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ style-colors/
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ styles/
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ styles-hash/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ cli/              # CLI commands
â”‚   â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ commands/     # Command implementations
â”‚   â”‚   â””â”€â”€ workers/      # Background workers
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ algolia/          # Algolia search integration
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ cache/            # Redis caching
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ health/           # Health checks
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ mms/              # MMS integration
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ mms-styles-sync/  # MMS style synchronization
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ newrelic/         # New Relic monitoring
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ env-config/       # Environment configuration
â”œâ”€â”€ dto/                  # Data Transfer Objects by feature
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ addons/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ brand-products/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ email-recommendations/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ frequently-bought-together/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ load-test/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ nlp-features/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ related-products/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ trending-products/
â”œâ”€â”€ utils/                # Utility functions
â”œâ”€â”€ middlewares/          # Custom middlewares
â”œâ”€â”€ interfaces/           # TypeScript interfaces
â”œâ”€â”€ decorators/           # Custom decorators
â”œâ”€â”€ interceptors/         # Custom interceptors
â”œâ”€â”€ guards/               # Auth guards
â”œâ”€â”€ constants/            # Constants and enums
â”œâ”€â”€ configs/              # Configuration files
â”œâ”€â”€ scripts/              # Automation & evaluation scripts
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ evaluator-related-products/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ evaluator-fbt/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ load-testing/
â”œâ”€â”€ migrations/           # Database migrations
â”œâ”€â”€ seeds/                # Database seeds
â””â”€â”€ unit-tests/          # Unit tests
test/                    # E2E tests
```

### Import Aliases (tsconfig.json)

```typescript
@config/*        â†’ src/configs/*
@constants/*     â†’ src/constants/*
@modules/*       â†’ src/modules/*
@utils/*         â†’ src/utils/*
@middlewares/*   â†’ src/middlewares/*
@services/*      â†’ src/services/*
@interfaces/*    â†’ src/interfaces/*
@decorators/*    â†’ src/decorators/*
@dto/*           â†’ src/dto/*
@guards/*        â†’ src/guards/*
@interceptors/*  â†’ src/interceptors/*
@frontend-tests/* â†’ src/frontend-tests/*
@seeds/*         â†’ src/seeds/*
```

### Database Models

The service includes comprehensive database models with full CRUD operations:

- **styles**: Core product styling data with UUID primary key
- **style_colors**: Color information (style_id, color_id, color_rgb, color_name)
- **styles_hash**: Hash-based style identification (style_id, hash, hashing_algorithm)
- **condensed_features**: Compressed feature data (style_id, condensed_features JSON)
- **category_correlations**: Category relationship data (primary_category_id, edge_id, edge_weight)
- **root_categories_correlations**: Root-level category relationships
- **forecasted_trending_products**: ML-forecasted trending products
- **product_annual_sales**: Annual sales data for products
- **promotional_products**: Promotional product data
- **redshift_orders**: Order data from Redshift
- **redshift_trending_items**: Trending items data from Redshift

Each model includes:

- Base, Create, and Update DTOs in `/dto/` folders or module folders
- TypeScript interfaces in `/interfaces/` folders
- Service layer with read/write database separation
- Soft deletes with `deleted_at` timestamp (where applicable)
- Proper indexing and unique constraints

### CLI Commands

Available CLI commands (via `npm run cli:dev`):

- **Migration Commands**: `migrate`, `migrate-up`, `migrate-down`, `migrate-rollback`
- **Data Population**: `populate-category-correlations`, `populate-trending-items`
- **Forecasting**: `forecast-trending-products`
- **Synchronization**: `sync-source-styles`
- **Data Preparation**: `prepare-promo-seed`

## Development Rules

### Code Style

- **NO COMMENTS**, **NO EXPLANATIONS** in code (except ESLint disable commands)
- **ALWAYS INSPECT EXISTING FILES** before creating new ones
- Match exact patterns from existing files:
  - `.model.ts` â†’ check `/src/modules/db/*/models/`
  - `.service.ts` â†’ check similar modules
  - `.controller.ts` â†’ check `/src/modules/rest/`
  - `.dto.ts` â†’ check `/src/dto/` or module folders
  - `.util.ts` â†’ check `/src/utils/`
  - `.unit-spec.ts` â†’ check `/src/unit-tests/`
  - `.e2e-spec.ts` â†’ check `/test/`
  - `.interface.ts` â†’ check `/src/interfaces/`

### Environment Variables (CRITICAL)

**ALWAYS follow this complete process when adding new environment variables:**

#### Step 1: Add to Environment Files

Add the new variable to env files with documentation:

```bash
# .env
NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
```

```bash
# .env.test
NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
```

```bash
# .env.example
NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=default_value_or_placeholder # Description of what this variable does
```

```bash
# .env.test.example
NEW_VARIABLE_NAME=test_value # Use appropriate test value
```

#### Step 2: Create Constant Export

Add constant to `src/constants/env-variables.constant.ts`:

```typescript
export const NEW_VARIABLE_NAME = 'NEW_VARIABLE_NAME';
```

#### Step 3: Add Validation Rule

Add validation decorator to `src/configs/env-validations.config.ts` in the `EnvironmentVariables` class:

```typescript
@IsString() // Use appropriate validator: @IsString(), @IsNumber(), @IsBoolean(), @IsEnum()
@IsNotEmpty() // Add constraints: @Min(), @Max(), etc.
NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: string; // Use appropriate type
```

**Common validators:**

- `@IsString()` - String values
- `@IsNumber()` - Numeric values
- `@IsBoolean()` - Boolean values
- `@IsEnum(EnumType)` - Enum values
- `@IsNotEmpty()` - Required field
- `@Min(value)` / `@Max(value)` - Numeric constraints
- `@IsUrl()` - URL validation

#### Step 4: Add to Environment Loader

Add to `src/utils/get-env-file.util.ts` in the `getEnvVariables()` function:

```typescript
export const getEnvVariables = (): Record<string, string | number | undefined> => ({
	// ... existing variables
	NEW_VARIABLE_NAME: process.env['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
});
```

#### Step 5: Add Test Coverage

Add test cases to `test/env-config.module.e2e-spec.ts`:

```typescript
// In beforeAll hook
testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'] = String(envConfig['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME']);

// In test case
it('should load NEW_VARIABLE_NAME correctly', () => {
	expect(envConfigService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME })).toBe(
		testEnvVariables['NEW_VARIABLE_NAME'],
	);
});
```

#### Usage: Two Approaches

**Approach 1: EnvConfigService (Dependency Injection)**

```typescript
import { Injectable } from '@nestjs/common';
import { EnvConfigService } from '@modules/env-config/env-config.service';
import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';

@Injectable()
export class MyService {
	constructor(private readonly configService: EnvConfigService) {}

	someMethod(): void {
		const value = this.configService.get<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
		const numericValue = this.configService.get<number>({
			key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME,
			converter: Number,
		});
	}
}
```

**Approach 2: getEnv Utility (Standalone Usage)**

```typescript
import { getEnv } from '@utils/get-env-variable.util';
import { NEW_VARIABLE_NAME } from '@constants/env-variables.constant';

const value = getEnv<string>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME });
const numericValue = getEnv<number>({ key: NEW_VARIABLE_NAME, converter: Number });
```

**Important Notes:**

- **ALWAYS** complete ALL 5 steps - missing any step will cause validation failures
- **NEVER** access `process.env` directly in application code
- **ALWAYS** use constants from `@constants/env-variables.constant`
- **TEST** your changes by running `npm run test:e2e`
- Validation runs automatically on app startup and will fail fast if misconfigured
- For production, environment variables are loaded from system environment
- For dev/test, variables are loaded from `.env` and `.env.test` files

### Git Workflow

- **Branches**: `RECO-XXXX-suffix`
- **Commits**: `RECO-XXXX: Description in imperative mood`
- **PRs**: `RECO-XXXX: Short description`, requires Code Owner approval, squash merge only

### Common Patterns

```typescript
// Logging
import { Logger } from '@nestjs/common';
export class ClassName {
  private readonly logger = new Logger(ClassName.name);
}

// Swagger
@ApiOperation({ summary: 'Description' })
@ApiResponse({ status: HttpStatusCode.Ok, description: 'Success response' })
```

### LogExecution Decorator (CRITICAL)

**ALWAYS wrap important methods and 3rd party requests with `@LogExecution` decorator.**

The `@LogExecution` decorator provides comprehensive logging and monitoring for method execution:

- **Performance tracking**: Logs execution time with warnings if exceeds threshold
- **Parameter logging**: Logs method input parameters
- **Response logging**: Logs method output
- **Error tracking**: Logs errors and reports to Rollbar
- **Sensitive data masking**: Automatically masks passwords, tokens, auth keys
- **Custom formatters**: Optional custom formatters for params/responses

**Usage:**

```typescript
import { LogExecution } from '@decorators/log-execution.decorator';

@Injectable()
export class MyService {
	// Basic usage - logs everything with defaults
	@LogExecution()
	async myMethod(param: string): Promise<Result> {
		// method implementation
	}

	// With custom formatters (for large payloads or 3rd party responses)
	@LogExecution({
		paramFormatter: (params: { styleId: string }) => ({ styleId: params.styleId }),
		responseFormatter: () => '', // Don't log large response
	})
	async fetchFromThirdParty({ styleId }: { styleId: string }): Promise<LargeData> {
		// 3rd party API call
	}

	// With custom options
	@LogExecution({
		logParams: true,
		logResponse: false,
		logExecutionTime: true,
		logErrToRollbar: true,
		maskSensitiveData: true,
	})
	async sensitiveOperation(data: SensitiveData): Promise<void> {
		// sensitive operation
	}
}
```

**When to use:**

- âœ… All 3rd party API requests (Algolia, MMS, AWS, OpenAI, etc.)
- âœ… Important business logic methods
- âœ… Database operations that may be slow
- âœ… Methods that handle sensitive data
- âœ… Methods with complex error scenarios
- â‌Œ Simple getters/setters
- â‌Œ Private utility methods
- â‌Œ Methods called in tight loops

**Configuration options:**

- `logParams`: Log method parameters (default: `true`)
- `logResponse`: Log method response (default: `true`)
- `logExecutionTime`: Log execution time (default: `true`)
- `logErrToRollbar`: Log errors to Rollbar (default: `true`)
- `rollbarLevel`: Rollbar log level (default: `'debug'`)
- `maskSensitiveData`: Mask sensitive keys (default: `true`)
- `sensitiveKeys`: Array of sensitive key names to mask
- `customContext`: Additional context to include in logs
- `paramFormatter`: Custom formatter function for parameters
- `responseFormatter`: Custom formatter function for response

**Default sensitive keys masked:**
`password`, `pass`, `token`, `secret`, `auth`, `authorization`, `cookie`, `session`, `credentials`, `apiKey`, `_cookie`, `csrf`, `_csrf`

## Configuration Files

### TypeScript Configuration (tsconfig.json)

- **Target**: ES2023 with strict type checking
- **Strict options**: All enabled including `noUncheckedIndexedAccess`, `exactOptionalPropertyTypes`
- **Path aliases**: Configured for clean imports (see Import Aliases section)
- **Incremental compilation**: Enabled for faster builds
- **strictPropertyInitialization**: Disabled (due to NestJS dependency injection)

### ESLint Configuration (eslint.config.mjs)

**When writing code, follow these ESLint rules:**

- **No console**: Use Logger instead of `console.log`
- **No relative imports**: Use path aliases only (`@modules/`, `@utils/`, etc.)
- **Explicit return types**: All functions must have explicit return types (`@typescript-eslint/explicit-module-boundary-types`)
- **Import order**: Enforced with newlines between groups (builtin â†’ external â†’ internal â†’ type)
- **TypeScript strict**: All strict TypeScript rules enabled
- **Test files**: Relaxed rules for `*.spec.ts` files (allow `any`, relative imports)
- **Import newlines**: Required after imports (`import/newline-after-import`)
- **No duplicates**: Duplicate imports not allowed (`import/no-duplicates`)

## Testing

- **Unit**: `*.unit-spec.ts` in `/src/unit-tests/`
- **E2E**: `*.e2e-spec.ts` in `/test/`
- **Coverage**: Separate reports in `/coverage-unit/` and `/coverage-e2e/`
- **DB**: MySQL replica with auto-reset for e2e tests
- **Load Testing**: Artillery configuration in `/src/scripts/load-testing/`

### Coverage Requirements

All test suites must maintain **95% minimum coverage** across all metrics:

- **Branches**: 95%
- **Functions**: 95%
- **Lines**: 95%
- **Statements**: 95%

**Configuration:**

- Coverage thresholds enforced in `test/jest.coverage.config.ts`
- Unit tests ignore `/modules/db/` patterns
- E2E tests collect coverage only from `*.(controller|service).ts` files
- Tests will fail if coverage falls below thresholds
- Run `npm run test:coverage` to verify all coverage metrics

## Key Dependencies

### Core

- **@nestjs/\*** - NestJS framework and modules
- **@nestjs/platform-fastify** - Fastify adapter for performance
- **knex** + **mysql2** - Database query builder and MySQL driver
- **nest-knexjs** - Knex integration for NestJS

### Caching & Queue

- **ioredis** + **@keyv/redis** - Redis client and keyv adapter
- **redlock** - Distributed locking
- **@aws-sdk/client-sqs** + **sqs-consumer** - AWS SQS integration

### Monitoring & Logging

- **newrelic** - Application performance monitoring
- **rollbar** - Error tracking
- **correlation-id-ts** - Request correlation tracking

### AI & Search

- **openai** - OpenAI API integration
- **algoliasearch** - Algolia search integration

### Utilities

- **class-transformer** + **class-validator** - DTO validation and transformation
- **color-diff** - Color comparison utilities
- **date-holidays** - Holiday date calculations

Based on my search, here are the relevant CLAUDE.md files for PR #348:

**List of CLAUDE.md files found:**

1. `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/recommendations-service/CLAUDE.md`

This is the only CLAUDE.md file in the repository. All modified files in PR #348 are located within the root `recommendations-service` directory, and they all reference this single root-level CLAUDE.md file for project guidance.
